# Yalo Chat SDK — Design Guidelines (Full Content)

> Design documentation for the Yalo Chat SDK. Covers the chat shell anatomy, widget components, theming, copy rules, and the process for creating new widgets. Intended for designers, engineers, sales teams, and AI agents building chat experiences across Android, iOS, Flutter, and Web.

Source: https://agentexperiencehub.netlify.app/sdk/welcome
GitHub: https://github.com/yalochat/chat-sdk


---

# Welcome

## Purpose & Scope

This documentation defines the design guidelines for creating, extending, and customizing widgets in the Yalo Chat SDK. It is intended to be used by designers, engineers, sales teams, and AI agents to produce consistent, high-quality chat experiences across all deployments and platforms.

**What is a widget?** A widget is a self-contained, interactive UI component that lives inside the chat window. Widgets are sent by the agent or the conversational flow to collect information, display content, or guide the user through a task. They are not free-form messages — they have a defined structure, behavior, and set of configurable properties.

**Scope:** All widgets in the chat-sdk repository across Android, iOS, Flutter, and Web platforms.

## Plug-and-Play Principle

The SDK ships with a library of pre-built widgets. Implementations do not build widgets from scratch — they configure them.

A widget is activated by sending a structured event with the widget type and its configuration — fields, labels, options, theme. The SDK handles rendering across Android, iOS, and Web.

Configuration → SDK → Renders everywhere.

## Core UX Philosophy

These principles are inherited from the Yalo Design System and adapted to the chat context. Every widget must follow all of them.

1. **Progressive Disclosure** — Show only what the user needs at each moment. Never front-load all information if the user doesn't need it yet. The Conversational Questions widget is the clearest expression of this — one question at a time.

2. **Simplicity Above All** — The chat window is a constrained space. Every element in a widget must earn its place. Each widget should answer one question: what should the user do right now?

3. **Predict User Actions** — Pre-fill fields when context is available. Show inline hints at decision points. Place the primary action where the user expects it — bottom of the widget.

4. **Consistency Over Novelty** — All widgets share the same visual language: spacing, border radius, typography, color roles. Do not introduce new visual patterns unless absolutely necessary.

5. **Forgiveness & Recovery** — Users make mistakes. Widgets must validate inline, show clear error messages, and never dead-end the user. If submission fails, the user must be able to correct and retry without losing their input.

6. **Accessibility First** — All widgets must be operable via keyboard and screen reader. Interactive elements must have visible focus states. Color must never be the only indicator of state. Minimum contrast: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI elements.

7. **Performance Is UX** — A widget that takes time to respond must communicate that wait. Use loading or skeleton states. Never leave the user wondering if their action registered. Use the Thinking widget whenever the expected wait exceeds 2 seconds.


---

# Design Widgets

## White-label by Design

Every deployment of the SDK can look and feel like a completely different product. The SDK provides the structure; the brand provides the identity.

### The Three Layers

**Structure** (owned by the SDK)
Layout, spacing, interaction behavior, states, and accessibility. Not customizable. This is what makes all widgets feel consistent.

**Theme** (configurable per deployment)
Primary color, border radius, font family, and button style. Applied via a theme config at the SDK level — one theme covers all widgets.

**Copy** (configurable per widget)
Titles, labels, placeholder text, button labels, and error messages.

### Configurable per deployment

| Property | Applies to |
|---|---|
| Primary color | Buttons, active states, highlights |
| Border radius | All cards and input fields |
| Font family | All text within widgets |
| Button style | Filled or outlined |
| Agent avatar & name | Shown in the chat header |

### Not configurable (enforced by the SDK)

- Layout and spacing of widget zones (header, content, action, footer)
- Interaction behavior (validation logic, navigation patterns, step progression)
- Accessibility properties (focus states, contrast requirements, touch target sizes)
- State behavior (error, loading, disabled)

## Widget Anatomy

All widgets share a common structure. This ensures visual consistency and predictable behavior across all widget types.

| Zone | Optional | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Header | Optional | Optional for display widgets. Required for input widgets. |
| Content Area | Required | Primary content — fields, cards, image. |
| Action Zone | Required | Primary button (Submit / Continue). Full width, always bottom-aligned. |
| Footer | Optional | Reserved for legal or attribution copy only. |

Maximum widget height: 80% of the visible chat window. Taller content scrolls within the widget.

## Checklist for Creating a New Widget

A widget should only be created if the required experience cannot be achieved through configuration of an existing widget.

### Definition
- Does a clear, specific use case exist that no current widget covers?
- Is this use case likely to recur across multiple deployments (not a one-off)?
- Has the widget been given a clear, descriptive name?
- Is the widget's purpose describable in one sentence?

### Structure
- Does the widget follow the standard anatomy (Header / Content / Action Zone / Footer)?
- Has a maximum content size been defined to prevent overflow?
- Does the widget support all relevant states: empty, filled, loading, error, disabled, read-only (post-submit)?

### Input & Interaction
- Are the supported input types listed (if applicable)?
- Are validation rules defined (required fields, formats, character limits)?
- Is the post-submit behavior defined (collapse, summary, next step)?

### Decision Rules
- Have the "use when" conditions been written?
- Have the "do not use when" conditions been written?
- Has the widget been added to the Decision Tree?

### Copy
- Are all user-facing strings listed (title, labels, placeholders, button, errors)?
- Has the copy been reviewed against the Copy Guidelines?
- Are translations available for all target deployment languages?

### Theming
- Are any additional themeable properties identified beyond the global theme?
- Are the non-customizable properties documented?

### Accessibility
- Do all interactive elements have visible focus states?
- Is color paired with another indicator (icon, text, pattern) for all state changes?
- Are all touch targets a minimum of 44×44px?


---

# Chat Window

The shell that wraps every conversation — header, message area, quick replies, and composer.

## Header

Agent branding, identity, and primary navigation actions.

| Element | Configurable | Hideable | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| background | Yes | No | Default #f1f5fc via --yalo-chat-header-background |
| Agent avatar | Yes | Optional | Circular. Flutter: chatIconImage. Web: image |
| Agent name | Yes | No | Required. Set via channelName |
| Connected status | Yes | Optional | Reactive to connection state. E.g. "Online" |
| Close / back button | Yes | No | Always present. Color via --yalo-chat-close-btn-color |
| Cart icon | Yes | Optional | Flutter: onCartPressed. Shows badge with item count. |
| Shop / filters icon | Yes | Optional | Flutter: onShopPressed |

## Messages

Main conversation area. Renders text bubbles, agent messages, and inline widgets.

| Element | Configurable | Hideable | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| User bubble | Yes | No | Right-aligned, max 75%. Default #F9FAFC. Flutter: userMessageColor |
| Agent message | Yes | No | Left-aligned, no bubble. Renders HTML/markdown. Flutter: assistantMessageTextStyle |
| Typing indicator | No | No | 3-dot animated loader. Handled automatically by the SDK. |
| In-chat widgets | Yes | Optional | ProductCarousel, ProductList, PromoCard, Suggestions. Rendered inline as agent messages. |

## Quick Replies

Contextual suggestion chips shown above the composer. Collapse automatically on scroll.

| Element | Configurable | Hideable | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Chips | Yes | Optional | Flutter: quickReplyColor / quickReplyBorderColor / quickReplyStyle |
| Scroll behavior | No | No | Collapse automatically when the user scrolls up or focuses the input. |
| Chip count | Yes | No | Controlled by the flow. |

## Composer

Text input with voice, attachment, and send controls.

| Element | Configurable | Hideable | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Text field | Yes | No | Border 1px solid #e8e8e8, rounded. Auto-resize up to 3 lines (web). |
| Placeholder | Yes | No | Flutter: hintTextStyle |
| Send button | Yes | No | Circular, default #2207F1. Flutter: sendButtonColor / sendButtonIcon |
| Mic / voice note | Yes | Optional | Shown when input is empty. Icon: recordAudioIcon |
| Camera / attachment | Yes | Optional | Flutter: showAttachmentButton (default true). Hidden during audio recording. |


---

# Customization

CSS custom properties and Flutter ChatTheme props for styling the chat window.

## Web — CSS Custom Properties

| Variable | Applies to | Default |
|---|---|---|
| --yalo-chat-header-background | Header background | #f1f5fc |
| --yalo-chat-header-color | Header text | #010101 |
| --yalo-chat-close-btn-color | Close / back button | #010101 |

## Flutter — ChatTheme

| Prop | Type | Zone | Default |
|---|---|---|---|
| backgroundColor | Color | Messages | #ffffff |
| appBarBackgroundColor | Color | Header | theme primary |
| userMessageColor | Color | Messages | #F9FAFC |
| inputTextFieldColor | Color | Composer | #ffffff |
| inputTextFieldBorderColor | Color | Composer | #e8e8e8 |
| sendButtonColor | Color | Composer | #2207F1 |
| sendButtonForegroundColor | Color | Composer | #ffffff |
| sendButtonIcon | Widget | Composer | send icon |
| recordAudioIcon | Widget | Composer | mic icon |
| chatIconImage | ImageProvider | Header | null (hidden) |
| quickReplyColor | Color | Quick Replies | #F9FAFC |
| quickReplyBorderColor | Color | Quick Replies | #ECEDEF |
| quickReplyStyle | TextStyle | Quick Replies | 14px, medium |
| userMessageTextStyle | TextStyle | Messages | 14px, regular |
| assistantMessageTextStyle | TextStyle | Messages | 14px, regular |
| hintTextStyle | TextStyle | Composer | 14px, #7c8086 |


---

# Widget Components

## Product Card Horizontal

Product card with a side image, promotional price, unit price, and quantity controls for cases and units.

**Use when:** You need to show a single product with detailed pricing and dual quantity controls (cases + units).

**Key props:**
- name — product name
- units — pack description (e.g. "24 units")
- price — promotional price
- originalPrice — crossed-out original price (optional)
- unitPrice — price per individual unit (optional)
- image — product image URL
- counters — array of { label, initial } for each counter (e.g. cases, units)
- currency — currency symbol (default "$")

---

## Product Card Vertical

Compact product card with a top image, price, minimum order label, and a single quantity counter.

**Use when:** You need a compact card for carousels or grids where horizontal space is limited.

**Key props:**
- name — product name
- price — promotional price
- originalPrice — crossed-out original price (optional)
- minOrderLabel — minimum order text (e.g. "From 5 cases")
- image — product image URL
- counterLabel — label for the quantity counter (default "units")
- initialQty — starting quantity (default 0)
- currency — currency symbol (default "$")

---

## Product List

Vertical list of Product Card Horizontals with dividers and a "See all" button.

**Constraints:** Min 2, max 5 products.

**Use when:** The agent is presenting a curated shortlist of products for the user to review and add to cart.

**Key props:**
- products — array of product objects (same shape as Product Card Horizontal props)
- onSeeAll — callback for "See all" button (optional; hides button if not provided)

---

## Product Carousel

Horizontal carousel of Product Card Verticals. Arrows on desktop, drag on mobile.

**Constraints:** Min 3, max 5 products.

**Use when:** You need to show a browsable selection of products without taking up full vertical space.

**Key props:**
- products — array of product objects (same shape as Product Card Vertical props)

---

## Suggestions

Conversation starter cards with an icon, title, description, and clickable suggestion chips. Displayed as a horizontal carousel.

**Constraints:** Min 1, max 3 cards.

**Use when:** Starting a conversation and you want to surface common intents before the user types anything.

**Key props (per card):**
- icon — image URL for the card icon
- title — card heading
- description — supporting text
- suggestions — array of { id, label } chips
- onSuggestionClick — callback when a chip is tapped

---

## Promo Card

Clickable full-width image banner with rounded corners.

**Use when:** You want to surface a promotion, announcement, or campaign image that the user can tap to navigate.

**Key props:**
- image — image URL
- alt — accessible description of the image
- href — navigation target (URL or deep link)
